message:
Ever notice how your dog will turn around two, three, sometimes five times before finally flopping down in their bed? 🐶 There's a surprisingly ancient reason behind that little bedtime ritual.

Long before cozy dog beds existed, your dog's wild ancestors had to make their own. Circling let them pat down tall grass, push aside sticks and stones, scare off any bugs, and check the area for anything unwelcome before settling in for the night. Some experts think the turning also helped them line up with the wind, so they could catch the scent of approaching danger even while they slept.

Your pup has a memory-foam bed and a climate-controlled house now — but that nest-building instinct is still written deep in their DNA. So those few spins before bed are really thousands of years of "getting it just right." 💛

How many turns does YOUR dog do before lying down? Be honest — do they have a magic number? 🐾
